Case Studies

Developer Tooling · OS Interface

Mission Control

Problem: Developers context-switch between 12+ tools daily. Every switch costs focus, flow, and time.

Key Decision: A unified, keyboard-first OS-style interface that aggregates terminal, file ops, AI completions, task management, and system metrics into a single cinematic dashboard.

Outcome: Personal productivity increase of 3× context retention.

Systems · Developer Tooling

Vim Plugin Marketplace

Problem: The Neovim plugin ecosystem has 3,000+ plugins with no unified discovery layer.

Key Decision: A full-stack marketplace built on a custom Lua plugin architecture with conflict detection, compatibility scoring, and semantic search.

Outcome: 150+ plugins indexed at launch.

Experience

Independent Builder

Self-directed

Full-time · Open Source

2023 – Present

Designing and shipping full-stack products end-to-end, from system architecture to production deployment. Focus on developer tooling, AI systems, and performance-first web experiences.

  • Shipped 6 production projects spanning AI, CLI tooling, and SaaS analytics
  • Maintained 3 open source repositories with combined 500+ stars
  • Established personal design system and component library used across all projects
  • Built and deployed on Vercel Edge with consistent Lighthouse 95+ scores

AI/ML Research Assistant

Atlas Skilltech University

Academic · Part-time

Jan 2025 – Apr 2025

Assisted faculty research in NLP and document processing pipelines. Contributed to a student-led RAG implementation benchmarking study.

  • Benchmarked 4 RAG retrieval strategies on 200-document academic corpus
  • Implemented custom chunking heuristics improving retrieval recall by 14%
  • Co-authored internal technical report adopted for departmental curriculum

Freelance Frontend Developer

Independent Clients

Freelance · Contract

Jun 2024 – Dec 2024

Delivered 4 client projects including SaaS landing pages, e-commerce storefronts, and portfolio sites focused on performance, accessibility, and motion-first UI.

  • Delivered all 4 projects on-time and within scope
  • Average Lighthouse score of 97 across delivered projects
  • Implemented motion design system reused across 3 client sites
  • 2 clients returned for follow-on projects
Next.jsPythonTypeScriptVercelDockerLangChainHuggingFacePineconeFastAPIFramer MotionTailwind CSSNext.jsPythonTypeScriptVercelDockerLangChainHuggingFacePineconeFastAPIFramer MotionTailwind CSS

How I Build

01

Ship to learn, not to finish.

A deployed project with rough edges teaches more than a perfect one that never leaves localhost. I bias toward momentum. Perfection is a v2 problem.

02

Design for the system, not the feature.

Features are temporary. Systems compound. Every architectural decision I make, I ask: does this generalize? Does this reduce future decisions?

03

Performance is product.

Every millisecond of latency is a UX failure. Every layout shift is a trust leak. I treat Lighthouse scores as product KPIs, not developer vanity.

04

Motion is communication.

Animation is not decoration. It is the language through which interfaces express causality, hierarchy, and meaning. I animate with intention or not at all.

05

Own the full stack, respect the specialist.

I build across the entire product surface because understanding how things connect makes me a better engineer at every layer. But I know when to defer to someone who goes deeper.

06

Taste is a technical skill.

The ability to recognize what is good, precise, and elegant — in code, in design, in architecture — is learnable, refinable, and one of the highest-leverage skills a builder can have.

Precision·Performance·Ownership

Ship fast, iterate faster, never compromise on quality.

Measure everything. Trust data over intuition.

Own the outcome, not just the task.

Technical Playground

Live Code

pipeline.ts
TypeScript
async function buildPipeline(config: PipelineConfig) {
  const steps = await loadSteps(config.manifest);
  const dag = compileDAG(steps);

  return dag.execute({
    parallel: true,
    retry: { maxAttempts: 3, backoff: 'exponential' },
    onProgress: (step) => emit('progress', step),
  });
}
